DocGuard AI User Guide

Version: v1.2.0 RC1 U6.1

Audience: end users, reviewers, analysts, records teams, and document owners

Purpose: step-by-step guidance for scanning, cleaning, verifying, reporting, and using the Writing Assistant

Current Release Update

DocGuard AI RC1 U6.1 is production-release approved. The current desktop experience uses the approved workflow navigation:

1. Dashboard

2. File Review

3. Findings

4. Writing Assistant

5. Risk Map

6. Cleanroom

7. Reports

8. Activity

9. Extensions

10. Settings

The latest UI fixes include:

  • Dashboard card drill-downs now open populated detail pages.
  • The risk distribution bar has improved spacing for low-count severity segments.
  • Selected findings remain readable while highlighted.
  • Corrupted display characters were removed from the dashboard and navigation surfaces.
  • Writing Assistant is included in Core and is not a separately licensed Professional feature.

Use Run Cleanroom for the end-to-end workflow that generates Reports and Activity.

1. What DocGuard AI Does

DocGuard AI helps you inspect and clean documents before sharing, filing, publishing, or releasing them. It runs locally on your workstation and helps identify metadata, comments, image data, document structure issues, PDF artifacts, and other findings that may need review.

DocGuard AI also includes a core Writing Assistant for heuristic writing analysis, sentence heatmaps, and rewrite assistance.

2. Before You Start

Before running a cleanup workflow:

1. Confirm you are working on the correct file or folder.

2. Confirm you have permission to inspect and clean the content.

3. Make a copy of important files if your team requires a separate working copy.

4. Keep Backup enabled unless your administrator tells you otherwise.

5. Use Dry Run first when working with critical or unfamiliar files.

6. Plan to review the verification result before sharing cleaned output.

3. Launch DocGuard AI

1. Open the Start menu, desktop shortcut, or portable folder.

2. Launch DocGuardAI.exe.

3. Confirm the window title shows DocGuard AI v1.2.0 RC1 U6.1.

4. Confirm the left sidebar is visible.

The main workflow across the top is:

1. Source

2. Scan

3. Review

4. Clean

5. Verify

6. Report

Work through the sidebar in this order for the clearest experience:

1. Dashboard

2. File Review

3. Findings

4. Writing Assistant, if needed

5. Risk Map

6. Cleanroom

7. Reports

8. Activity

9. Extensions

10. Settings

4. Choose A Source

1. Click Browse.

2. Select a folder or file.

3. Confirm the selected path appears in the Source field.

4. Leave Recursive enabled if you want subfolders included.

5. Clear Recursive if you only want the selected folder level.

Choose a single file when you need to inspect one document. Choose a folder when preparing a group of files for delivery or review.

5. Understand Supported Files

Core supported file families include:

  • TXT
  • Markdown
  • HTML
  • DOCX
  • PDF
  • PPTX
  • XLSX and XLSM
  • JPEG
  • PNG
  • TIFF
  • WEBP

Enabled module packs add inspection support for email, CAD, and media formats. Some of those formats are inspection-first and may provide remediation guidance rather than direct cleaning.

6. Select A Profile

Use the Profile selector before scanning or cleaning.

| Profile | Use This When |

|---|---|

| standard | You want conservative cleanup for routine metadata reduction. |

| aggressive | You are preparing files for normal release or external sharing. |

| paranoid | You need the strongest cleanup and have time to verify output carefully. |

Recommended first choice: aggressive.

Use paranoid carefully. It can apply stronger document handling where supported, so review output before sending or filing cleaned documents.

7. Set Safety Controls

Review the safety controls before running a workflow.

| Control | Recommended Setting | Meaning |

|---|---|---|

| Backup | On | Creates backups before supported file changes. |

| Recursive | On for folders | Includes subfolders. |

| Dry Run | On for preview, off for actual cleaning | Shows what would happen without modifying files. |

| Typography | Optional | Normalizes selected typography markers where supported. |

| Remove EXIF | On | Removes image EXIF metadata where supported. |

Recommended first pass:

1. Turn Backup on.

2. Turn Recursive on if scanning a folder.

3. Turn Dry Run on for the first review.

4. Select aggressive.

5. Click Scan.

8. Run A Scan

Use Scan when you want to inspect before changing anything.

1. Select a source.

2. Select a profile.

3. Set safety controls.

4. Click Scan.

5. Wait for progress to complete.

6. Review the Dashboard metrics.

7. Open File Review and Findings for details.

After scanning, DocGuard AI populates:

  • Dashboard cards
  • Risk distribution
  • Top risk files
  • File Review tree
  • Findings table
  • Risk Map
  • Recent activity

9. Review The Dashboard

The Dashboard page summarizes the current source and latest run.

Review:

  • Files scanned
  • Files cleaned
  • Total findings
  • Findings removed
  • Highest risk
  • Verification failures
  • Risk distribution
  • Top risk files
  • Pipeline verification
  • Recent activity

If Verification Failures is greater than zero after a full pipeline, review Reports and File Review before using the output.

10. Review Details In File Review

1. Open File Review.

2. Expand the file tree.

3. Select a file to view file-level details.

4. Select a category to view category details.

5. Select an individual finding to view the finding detail and remediation guidance.

Use File Review when you need to answer:

  • Which file caused the risk?
  • Which category was detected?
  • What does the finding mean?
  • What remediation is recommended?
  • Is the file clean or does it have residual findings?

11. Review Findings

1. Open Findings.

2. Review high-risk rows first.

3. Check file path, category, severity, score, finding name, and remediation.

4. Decide whether to clean, document residual risk, or remove the file from the release set.

Findings can include:

  • metadata
  • comments
  • EXIF
  • document structure
  • PDF metadata
  • PDF structure
  • image metadata
  • image structure
  • errors

12. Use The Risk Map

1. Open Risk Map.

2. Review files by risk band.

3. Prioritize high-risk files.

4. Return to File Review or Findings for detail.

The heatmap is useful when scanning a large folder and deciding where to spend review time.

13. Clean Files

Use Clean when you are ready to modify supported files.

1. Confirm the source path is correct.

2. Confirm Backup is on.

3. Turn off Dry Run if you want actual changes.

4. Select the profile.

5. Click Clean.

6. Wait for completion.

7. Review the Dashboard, Reports, and Activity.

If Dry Run is on, DocGuard AI previews the operation without changing files.

14. Run Cleanroom

Use Run Cleanroom for the recommended end-to-end workflow.

1. Select a source.

2. Select a profile.

3. Confirm safety controls.

4. Click Run Cleanroom.

5. Wait for the run to complete.

6. Review Pipeline Verification.

7. Open Reports.

8. Review Activity.

The full pipeline performs:


scan -> clean -> post-clean scan -> verify -> report -> Activity

Use this workflow when preparing a final release package or controlled document set.

15. Understand Verification

Verification compares findings before and after cleaning.

If verification passes:

  • cleaned files have no score-bearing residual findings in the verification result
  • reports and Activity are updated
  • the workflow can move to final review

If verification fails:

1. Open File Review.

2. Review the file with residual findings.

3. Open Reports.

4. Review the post-clean scan report.

5. Decide whether to retry with a stronger profile, perform manual remediation, or document residual risk.

Do not distribute files until verification failures are understood.

16. Open Reports

1. Open Reports.

2. Click Refresh Reports if needed.

3. Select a report.

4. Review the report in the viewer.

Full pipeline runs can generate:

  • pre-clean scan report
  • clean report
  • post-clean scan report

Reports are local files. Treat them as sensitive if they contain paths or finding details.

17. Review Activity

1. Open Activity.

2. Click Refresh Activity.

3. Review timestamp, mode, profile, scanned files, cleaned files, findings, removed findings, and highest risk.

Use Activity to confirm that a workflow completed and to review prior runs.

18. Use Cleanroom

The Cleanroom page provides a simple way to run the selected source through the pipeline.

1. Select a source.

2. Open Cleanroom.

3. Click Run Current Source.

4. Wait for completion.

5. Review Reports and Activity.

Cleanroom uses the same general order as the full pipeline and is useful when processing known source folders.

19. Use The Writing Assistant

The Writing Assistant is part of the core DocGuard AI package.

To analyze text:

1. Open Writing Assistant.

2. Paste text into the editor.

3. Review the live analysis.

4. Review Findings and Statistics tabs.

5. Review sentence heatmap output.

To rewrite text:

1. Paste text into the editor.

2. Select a style.

3. Click Rewrite.

4. Review the suggested rewrite.

5. Review changed words and applied changes.

6. Use Replace Original only after reviewing the rewritten text.

7. Use Copy to copy the rewrite.

Available styles:

  • natural
  • humanize
  • executive
  • technical
  • academic
  • government

Important: Writing Assistant output is heuristic and rule-based. It does not prove authorship, guarantee compliance, or replace human review.

20. Use Extensions

Open Extensions to view enabled Extensions.

Core Extensions include:

  • Document Sanitizer
  • Office Sanitizer
  • PDF Sanitizer
  • Image Sanitizer
  • Writing Assistant

Additional entries can include:

  • Word Add-in
  • Outlook Add-in
  • Browser Extension
  • CAD Pack
  • Email Pack
  • Media Pack

Some Extensions are inspection-first. If a report says manual remediation is required, follow your team process.

21. Use Settings And License Status

Open Settings to view product and license details.

Use the menu:


Tools -> License Status

Use this when:

  • your administrator asks for license status
  • activation state looks unexpected
  • you need to confirm whether DocGuard AI is in trial or activated mode

Do not send full product keys in screenshots or support requests.

22. Use Activity And Reports

Open Activity when you need a run-by-run summary. Open Reports when you need detailed evidence for review, audit, or release decisions.

Activity and Reports can help answer:

  • Did the run start?
  • Which stage is running?
  • Were reports generated?
  • Did verification pass or fail?
  • Was a file skipped or unsupported?

If you contact support, include the relevant Activity row, report path, or a screenshot of the issue.

23. Command Line Basics

Administrators may ask you to run command-line checks.

Scan:


sanitize scan "C:\Path\To\FileOrFolder" --profile aggressive --summary

Clean:


sanitize clean "C:\Path\To\FileOrFolder" --profile aggressive --html-report auto

Run Cleanroom:


sanitize run "C:\Path\To\FileOrFolder" --profile aggressive

View Activity:


sanitize history --last 20

List Extensions:


sanitize plugins

Writing Assistant:


sanitize analyze-text --text "Paste text here" --heatmap
sanitize rewrite --text "Paste text here" --style executive

24. Recommended End-To-End Workflow

For a document release set:

1. Create or identify the working copy of the files.

2. Launch DocGuard AI.

3. Click Browse.

4. Select the source folder.

5. Select aggressive.

6. Enable Backup.

7. Enable Recursive if needed.

8. Enable Dry Run.

9. Click Scan.

10. Review Dashboard.

11. Review File Review and Findings.

12. If results are expected, turn off Dry Run.

13. Click Run Cleanroom.

14. Review Pipeline Verification.

15. Open Reports.

16. Review the post-clean scan report.

17. Open Activity and confirm the run is recorded.

18. Retain reports according to your team policy.

19. Use the cleaned files only after verification has been reviewed.

25. Best Practices

  • Use a working copy for important documents.
  • Keep backups enabled.
  • Start with Scan before Clean.
  • Use Dry Run on unfamiliar file sets.
  • Review high-risk findings first.
  • Do not ignore verification failures.
  • Keep reports with the related case or release record.
  • Treat reports as sensitive.
  • Ask an administrator before using paranoid on important documents.
  • Review Writing Assistant output before using it.

26. Troubleshooting

| Issue | What To Do |

|---|---|

| No source selected | Click Browse and choose a file or folder. |

| Scan finds nothing | Confirm file type is supported and Recursive is set correctly. |

| Reports page is empty | Run Run Cleanroom or Cleanroom first. |

| Activity is empty | Run a scan, clean, or full pipeline first. |

| Clean did not change files | Check Dry Run, file type support, and whether findings are informational. |

| Verification failed | Review File Review and post-clean report before distributing files. |

| Writing Assistant is missing | Ask your administrator to confirm you are using the latest DocGuard AI package. |

| License looks wrong | Use Tools -> License Status and contact your administrator. |

| App does not launch | Reopen from the extracted portable folder or contact support. |

27. What To Send Support

When asking for help, include:

  • DocGuard AI version
  • Windows version
  • Whether you used GUI or CLI
  • Source file type, not sensitive content
  • Profile used
  • Safety settings used
  • Screenshot of the error, relevant Activity row, or report excerpt
  • Report path, if available
  • Whether the issue happens on a copied sample

Do not include confidential document content or full product keys unless your administrator explicitly approves that handling path.
